No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course title 'Phenomenology and Psychological Healing' and its core content, including 'understanding human suffering and healing' and 'end-of-life care', directly and centrally align with promoting mental health and well-being (Target 3.4).
evidence: 理解人之受苦與療癒的視野 (a perspective for understanding human suffering and healing)

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to provide students a path to 'know themselves' and shift from 'knowledge about things' to 'knowledge about oneself as a person', aligning with specific learning outcomes for personal development (Target 4.7).
evidence: 提供學生「認識自己」的現象學徑路 (provide students a phenomenological path to 'know themselves')
從「關於物的知識學習」轉換到「關於自己做為人的知識學習」 (shift from 'knowledge learning about things' to 'knowledge learning about oneself as a person')

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's focus on understanding 'consciousness shared by all human beings' can indirectly foster empathy and social inclusion, contributing minimally to reducing inequalities (Target 10.2).
evidence: 理解自身做為人所共有的意識作用特性 (understand... the characteristics of consciousness shared by all human beings)

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course's critique of 'objectifying' oneself, viewing oneself as an 'object', can be minimally interpreted as challenging materialistic perspectives, aligning with sustainable lifestyles (Target 12.8).
evidence: 大部分人也經常「物化」自己 (most people also often 'objectify' themselves)
